hypothesis: Training 3D molecular generative models with corrected valency calculations and Kekulized, chemically accurate datasets yields generated structures that are significantly closer to GFN2-xTB–optimized geometries (lower bond length, bond angle, and torsion deviations and lower relaxation energies) than models trained on the original dataset, while MMFF94-based evaluation systematically overestimates these geometry errors compared to GFN2-xTB.
